headline: Agent orchestration at scale and the Model Context Protocol break out alongside a wave of Claude Fable 5 coverage.
body: Multi-agent orchestration is the dominant thread: Cursor describes running 128 coding agents at once with inter-agent communication and review workflows, while subagents and parallel execution both break out as tools. The Model Context Protocol and JSON-RPC enter as new techniques, surfacing in a Google Cloud tutorial that wires agents to Looker and enterprise data. Claude Fable 5 drives much of the new material, spanning Claude Code tutorials, automation pipelines, and a growing backlash over aggressive safety filters that refuse harmless prompts and raise questions about hidden guardrails and fallback models. Tooling for grounded data access also emerges, with query validation, schema injection, and HTML generation appearing through WorkOS Studio.

cited_items.title: Your first agent configuration with MCP and Looker
The Fable 5 Backlash Is Getting Serious
